jokes.text: Why is there hair everywhere? Did you shave in the kitchen? The water in here is softer.
jokes.setup: Hair everywhere in kitchen raises normal question about shaving
jokes.punchline: Dennis's oblivious logic about water softness
jokes.explanation: Good character moment establishing Dennis as obliviously inconsiderate, solid setup/payoff structure

jokes.text: Look at this ski-jumping idiot, Bode Miller. What kind of a fruity name is Bode?
jokes.setup: Dennis criticizing Olympic athlete
jokes.punchline: Ironic criticism of 'fruity' name from someone named Dennis
jokes.explanation: Standard character comedy, mildly funny irony but predictable

jokes.text: That cereal has the Olympics on it? It must be eight months old.
jokes.setup: Olympics imagery on cereal box
jokes.punchline: Logical deduction about cereal's age from Olympic timing
jokes.explanation: Clever observational humor with good economy and unexpected logic

jokes.text: When you and me have kids, we're gonna give them good names, like Shannon or Rick. When we what?
jokes.setup: Dennis casually planning their future children
jokes.punchline: Liz's horrified reaction to his assumption
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe comedy with excellent character work - Dennis's obliviousness vs Liz's horror

jokes.text: Look at this maze. It's so easy. You have to go in here, and out there.
jokes.setup: Dennis looking at what's presumably a complex maze
jokes.punchline: His oversimplified 'solution' that misses the point entirely
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Dennis's complete lack of understanding of basic concepts

jokes.text: Oh, Dennis. I just did my hair. Oh, that's my girl.
jokes.setup: Dennis wanting physical affection as Liz leaves
jokes.punchline: Liz's resignation and Dennis's oblivious satisfaction
jokes.explanation: Solid relationship comedy showing their dysfunction through small moments

jokes.text: I'm breaking up with my boyfriend tonight. I can't take it anymore. Hmm, 35, single, no children. Three sexual partners in the last ten years. I don't know, doll. Maybe it's time to settle.
jokes.setup: Liz confiding in doctor about relationship problems
jokes.punchline: Doctor's brutally clinical assessment suggesting she should settle
jokes.explanation: Dark, unexpected humor that perfectly captures dating anxiety and medical bedside manner

jokes.text: Hmm, 35, single, no children. Three sexual partners in the last ten years. I don't know, doll. Maybe it's time to settle.
jokes.setup: Doctor reviewing what should be medical information
jokes.punchline: Giving unsolicited relationship advice based on sexual history
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly inappropriate medical advice - subverts doctor-patient relationship expectations

jokes.text: Chocolate chip, or butter crunch?
jokes.setup: Serious medical consultation atmosphere
jokes.punchline: Doctor casually offering cookies as if this is normal medical practice
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist moment with excellent economy - completely unexpected in medical context

jokes.text: No, that's not true. What about that movie you did last summer? "The Rural Juror"? Yeah, The Rur-- That one.
jokes.setup: Pete trying to be supportive about Jenna's movie
jokes.punchline: His inability to pronounce the tongue-twister title
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay joke that becomes a running gag - impossible to say 'Rural Juror' clearly

jokes.text: Rural Juror, apple three, take one. Mark! A terrible title. Action!
jokes.setup: Movie scene being filmed
jokes.punchline: Director openly admitting the title is terrible while filming
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor and callback, director breaking fourth wall adds to the absurdity

jokes.text: I will tell him that you are in a feature film called The Rrr Jrr.
jokes.setup: Pete promising to tell Jack about Jenna's movie
jokes.punchline: His complete mangling of the already impossible title
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that escalates the pronunciation joke to pure gibberish

jokes.text: What the hell's her movie called? I don't know. She's been talking about it for a year. I can't ask her now.
jokes.setup: Writers confused about the movie title
jokes.punchline: They've been too embarrassed to ask for clarification for a year
jokes.explanation: Good callback that extends the joke's social awkwardness - relatable workplace comedy

jokes.text: I am a stabbing robot. I will stab you. Oh, jeez. All right, no, okay.
jokes.setup: Tracy as guest on talk show
jokes.punchline: His completely unhinged robot impression threatening violence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy Jordan moment - completely unpredictable and threatening yet somehow innocent

jokes.text: You know, I used to be very rich. Handsome? Yes. You find me handsome? Yes, very much.
jokes.setup: Tracy getting his hair done
jokes.punchline: Random confession about former wealth followed by fishing for compliments
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tracy's narcissism and need for validation

jokes.text: I know you're wearing that as a joke, but it makes you look younger, and more confident, and I think you should consider it.
jokes.setup: Pete wearing a wig as a joke
jokes.punchline: Liz's sincere assessment that it actually improves his appearance
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - what starts as obvious visual gag becomes sincere character moment

jokes.text: Because if I have a choice between an international movie star, and a woman who does commercials for ShopRite-- No, no, no. Jenna doesn't do those commercials anymore. She got fired.
jokes.setup: Jack dismissing Jenna for doing commercials
jokes.punchline: Liz's defense making it worse by revealing she was fired
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation joke where the defense makes the situation worse - perfect Liz moment

jokes.text: That's why my job is way better than yours. Way better.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining the corporate hierarchy
jokes.punchline: His petty emphasis on job superiority
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jack's petty corporate mentality with perfect delivery

jokes.text: That's what they said about Hasselhoff, then he tried to make out with me during a commercial break.
jokes.setup: Discussing Tracy's doctor's assurance about his behavior
jokes.punchline: Conan's traumatic Hasselhoff experience as counterexample
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd reference that perfectly captures talk show host nightmare scenarios

jokes.text: You do a live Christmas Eve special from Kabul every year until the war on terror is won. Tell Tracy I'll see him tonight. You, black Irish bastard. Back at you, man.
jokes.setup: Jack threatening Conan with consequences
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific military threat followed by ethnic banter
jokes.explanation: Fantastic escalation from corporate threat to absurd military assignment, plus perfect character rapport

jokes.text: Tell Tracy I'll see him tonight. You, black Irish bastard.
jokes.setup: Conan accepting Jack's terms
jokes.punchline: Affectionate insult referencing Jack's heritage
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing mutual respect through insults - very show business

jokes.text: Me and my wife like to play "rape." She go in the bathroom and do her hair. Then I put on a ski mask... Okay, not that.
jokes.setup: Writers asking Tracy for wholesome stories about his wife
jokes.punchline: Tracy's completely inappropriate overshare about role-playing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy moment - completely tone-deaf oversharing that horrifies the writers

jokes.text: I like to walk around my house naked, to remind my oldest son who's still got the biggest ding dong.
jokes.setup: Looking for family-friendly anecdotes about being a dad
jokes.punchline: Tracy's completely inappropriate competitive relationship with his son
jokes.explanation: Another perfect Tracy moment - turning fatherhood into bizarre competition

jokes.text: I can tell the story of how I met Sharon Stone. Oh, what was that? I was pooping in the ladies' room at the Ivy... No.
jokes.setup: Promising a glamorous celebrity encounter story
jokes.punchline: Revealing it involves him inappropriately using the ladies' room
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection from glamorous to completely inappropriate, perfect Tracy logic

jokes.text: My hair hurts. Anybody gonna answer the phone? What phone? Is anybody gonna answer the phone? The phone! It's ringing! Ring! Ring!
jokes.setup: Tracy making increasingly agitated statements
jokes.punchline: His paranoid insistence about a non-existent ringing phone
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Tracy's mental state deterioration, absurd complaints escalating to hallucinations

jokes.text: Is anybody gonna answer the phone? The phone! It's ringing! Ring! Ring!
jokes.setup: Tracy asking about a phone
jokes.punchline: No one else can hear a phone, Tracy making ringing sounds
jokes.explanation: Good escalating paranoia moment - Tracy hearing phones that aren't there

jokes.text: Lemon, this is not open mike night at the Bryn Mawr student union.
jokes.setup: Liz offering comedy suggestions for Jack's corporate event
jokes.punchline: Jack's dismissive reference to amateur college comedy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack insult combining elitism and comedy knowledge - very specific and cutting

jokes.text: For that kind of money this stuffed chicken breast better paint my house.
jokes.setup: Liz trying another joke about expensive dinner
jokes.punchline: Absurd expectation that food should perform manual labor
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke - absurd expectation that makes economic sense in a silly way

jokes.text: Jack Welch has such unparalleled management skills, they named Welch's grape juice after him. Because he squeezes the sweetest juice out of his workers' mind grapes.
jokes.setup: Jack proposing his own joke for the corporate dinner
jokes.punchline: Convoluted grape juice pun involving 'mind grapes'
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly bad corporate humor that perfectly captures Jack's disconnect from actual comedy

jokes.text: That doesn't even make sense. No, it doesn't, does it? I wrote it down in the middle of the night.
jokes.setup: Liz pointing out Jack's joke doesn't make sense
jokes.punchline: Jack's honest admission and explanation for its incoherence
jokes.explanation: Great meta moment acknowledging the absurdity while revealing character vulnerability

jokes.text: Cookie in the middle of the day? I gave blood. Does that burn calories?
jokes.setup: Jack noticing Liz eating cookies during the day
jokes.punchline: Liz's logical but naive question about blood donation and calories
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Liz's hopeful thinking about health and diet

jokes.text: I could talk about how the moon is a spy satellite put there by Oprah and minister Farrakhan. And not the minister Farrakhan you're thinking of.
jokes.setup: Tracy offering conspiracy theory material for talk show
jokes.punchline: Clarifying it's a different Farrakhan than expected
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist conspiracy theory with perfect Tracy twist - the clarification makes it even weirder

jokes.text: Who's that dude? Uh, what dude? The blue dude. Tell him to stop staring at me.
jokes.setup: Writers trying to have normal conversation with Tracy
jokes.punchline: Tracy's paranoid hallucination about a blue person
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Tracy's mental state with specific absurd hallucination

jokes.text: Meep.
jokes.setup: Normal workplace situation
jokes.punchline: Kenneth making an inexplicable cartoon sound
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth moment - completely random and innocent, maximum economy

jokes.text: Oh, that's not good. Frank, that was my blood cookie.
jokes.setup: Frank apparently eating Liz's cookie
jokes.punchline: Liz referring to it as 'blood cookie' from earlier blood donation
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier blood donation joke with perfect Liz possessiveness

jokes.text: Hey, Jack Attack. Sorry, I should have kept that one in the old brain box.
jokes.setup: Pete using casual nickname for Jack
jokes.punchline: Immediate realization it was inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Good Pete moment showing his social awkwardness and immediate regret

jokes.text: Pete, did you know that men with full heads of hair on average earn 17% more than their bald counterparts?
jokes.setup: Jack discussing Pete's wig wearing
jokes.punchline: Citing specific statistic about hair and income
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack moment - treating shallow appearance concerns with corporate precision

jokes.text: Perhaps it's because bald men are generally less informed than men with full heads of hair.
jokes.setup: Following up on hair/income statistic
jokes.punchline: Completely illogical correlation between hair and intelligence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jack logic - making completely absurd causal connections with total confidence

jokes.text: I'm just kidding. It's real. I'm not like you.
jokes.setup: Jack inviting Pete to pull his hair to prove it's real
jokes.punchline: Casually insulting Pete while confirming his own hair is natural
jokes.explanation: Great Jack cruelty disguised as casual observation - perfect corporate sociopathy

jokes.text: Well, you tell him that those commercials paid for my vacation home. So, unless he would like to buy me a condo in Clearwater, Florida...
jokes.setup: Jenna defending her commercial work
jokes.punchline: Escalating to specific real estate demands in Florida
jokes.explanation: Good Jenna moment showing her defensive materialism and specific geographic preferences

jokes.text: I'm bugging out! I'm bugging out. I'm bugging out!
jokes.setup: Tracy's mental state deteriorating
jokes.punchline: Repetitive panicked declaration of his mental break
jokes.explanation: Effective escalation of Tracy's breakdown with repetitive panic - sets up medical intervention

jokes.text: This is Dr. Leo Spa-che-men.
jokes.setup: Liz reading doctor's name from prescription
jokes.punchline: Pronunciation revealing 'Spaceman' is pronounced pretentiously
jokes.explanation: Brilliant name joke - 'Spaceman' pronounced as if it's sophisticated French name

jokes.text: But what can you do? Medicine's not a science.
jokes.setup: Doctor explaining Tracy's unpredictable drug interactions
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal of medicine as non-scientific
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dr. Spaceman line - completely undermines medical authority with casual absurdity

jokes.text: There's not really a name for what Tracy has. Basically it's erratic tendencies, and delusions, brought on by excessive notoriety. And certainly not helped by my wildly experimental treatments.
jokes.setup: Asking for Tracy's diagnosis
jokes.punchline: Doctor admitting he's making it up and experimenting wildly
jokes.explanation: Excellent Dr. Spaceman moment - casually admitting medical malpractice while describing made-up condition

jokes.text: No, he should be fine, so long as you keep him away from bright lights, loud music and crowds.
jokes.setup: Asking if Tracy is dangerous
jokes.punchline: Doctor listing everything that describes a TV taping
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic setup - doctor describes exactly what Tracy needs to avoid right before talk show

jokes.text: Uh...do you need anything for yourself?
jokes.setup: Medical consultation ending
jokes.punchline: Doctor offering drugs to random caller
jokes.explanation: Shows Dr. Spaceman's complete disregard for medical ethics. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: When I first met Jack Welch, I thought he was such a great golfer, he made Bob Darnell look like Randy Barnes.
jokes.setup: Jack practicing his corporate dinner joke
jokes.punchline: Completely nonsensical comparison using random names
jokes.explanation: Good Jack moment showing his complete disconnect from actual humor through meaningless references

jokes.text: 'The Rrr Jrr.' The what?
jokes.setup: Liz telling Jack about Jenna's movie
jokes.punchline: Jack can't understand the title pronunciation either
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback escalation - even reduced pronunciation is incomprehensible

jokes.text: Leo's an excellent physician. And a pretty good dentist.
jokes.setup: Defending Dr. Spaceman's medical competence
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning he also practices dentistry
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist detail about medical professional doubling as dentist - perfectly casual delivery

jokes.text: Who is this leader of men? What can I do for you, handsome?
jokes.setup: Frank encountering Pete in his wig
jokes.punchline: Completely changed demeanor treating him as attractive authority figure
jokes.explanation: Great visual gag callback showing how dramatically the wig changes perceptions

jokes.text: You're prettier than Deborah Messing. Please don't quit. Well, if that's the way you feel, I'll stay.
jokes.setup: Liz desperately trying to convince Jenna not to quit
jokes.punchline: Jenna immediately agreeing after being compared to TV star
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing both Jenna's vanity and Liz's manipulation skills

jokes.text: Call Ticketmaster right away. Nickleback just added another date, right? I would do it on your computer, but I'm downloading a game right now.
jokes.setup: Dennis calling Liz for a favor
jokes.punchline: Asking her to buy Nickelback tickets while he downloads games
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis moment - terrible musical taste and selfish priorities combined

jokes.text: Blue man! That blue dude keeps following me! Meek, eep.
jokes.setup: Tracy's continued paranoia about blue person
jokes.punchline: Kenneth making cartoon sounds in response
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Tracy's hallucination with Kenneth's innocent cartoon response

jokes.text: Meek, eep.
jokes.setup: Tracy continuing to make nonsense sounds
jokes.punchline: Different nonsense sounds
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier nonsense sounds but diminishing returns

jokes.text: You still going out with that guy from the pager store? Who, Dennis? Yeah. You still, um... How's your wife? Let's not do this, Elizabeth.
jokes.setup: Conan and Liz having awkward personal conversation
jokes.punchline: Both recognizing the inappropriateness and stopping
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment showing professional boundaries and personal history tension

jokes.text: Whoa, he licked all of those.
jokes.setup: Tracy presumably licking props or food
jokes.punchline: Disgusted observation about contamination
jokes.explanation: Good gross-out moment showing Tracy's drug-induced behavior

jokes.text: No! Past Pete is here to kill Future Pete! It's going great.
jokes.setup: Asking how Tracy is doing in the bathroom
jokes.punchline: Tracy's time-travel paranoia contrasted with Liz's sarcastic assessment
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist Tracy line with perfect Liz deadpan response - time travel paranoia is brilliantly random

jokes.text: Estate tax reform.
jokes.setup: Jack's proposed Thanksgiving joke for wealthy crowd
jokes.punchline: Assuming wealthy people are thankful for tax policy
jokes.explanation: Good Jack class awareness - perfectly calibrated to wealthy audience

jokes.text: It's after 6:00. What am I, a farmer?
jokes.setup: Questioning why Jack is wearing a tux
jokes.punchline: Jack's explanation that formal wear is required after 6 PM
jokes.explanation: Iconic Jack line showing his complete disconnect from normal life - perfect character-defining moment

jokes.text: You're gonna tell me my feet stink? You don't even have feet. Blue man! Blue man, where your feet at?
jokes.setup: Tracy continuing to hallucinate blue person
jokes.punchline: Logical argument about the blue person's lack of feet
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Tracy's absurd logic applied to his hallucination - feet observation is random but committed

jokes.text: Blue man, where your feet at?
jokes.setup: Tracy's ongoing blue man hallucination
jokes.punchline: Logical question about footless entity
jokes.explanation: Good callback with logical progression - questioning the anatomy of hallucination

jokes.text: Which...is okay, 'cause at least he didn't murder me.
jokes.setup: Tracy falling asleep during the interview
jokes.punchline: Conan's relief that this is better than being stabbed
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier stabbing incident with perfect deadpan delivery from talk show host perspective

jokes.text: So what did you want to talk to me about, huh? Nothing. I forget. I just want to listen to you play Halo 'til I fall asleep.
jokes.setup: Liz asking about the important conversation Dennis mentioned
jokes.punchline: Dennis's complete lack of memory and simple gaming desires
jokes.explanation: Perfect relationship comedy showing how Dennis builds up drama then has no follow-through

jokes.text: Well, I got started in the NBC page program. And before you know it, I'm making hit movies with my good friend, and roommate, Zach Braff.
jokes.setup: Kenneth presumably telling his life story
jokes.punchline: Claiming to be roommates with celebrity Zach Braff
jokes.explanation: Good Kenneth absurdity - completely random celebrity name-drop delivered with innocent confidence

jokes.text: Well, I got started in the NBC page program. And before you know it, I'm making hit movies with my good friend, and roommate, Zach Braff.
jokes.setup: Kenneth presumably on Conan after Tracy
jokes.punchline: Claiming friendship and cohabitation with random celebrity
jokes.explanation: Great Kenneth delusion - specific celebrity choice makes it funnier
